title: Crispy Battered Dutch Plant-based Fries
description: A Dutch twist on the classic French fry, featuring a crispy cornstarch-based batter infused with Chinese five spice powder and air-fried to perfection. Enjoy these addictive fries as a snack or side dish.

ingredients.name: Chinese five spice powder
ingredients.notes: use store-bought or homemade spice blend

ingredients.name: Vegan batter mix
ingredients.notes: depending on brand, choose one that is gluten-free and vegan-friendly

ingredients.name: Vegan egg replacement
ingredients.notes: such as Ener-G Egg Replacer or flaxseed

ingredients.name: Vegan milk
ingredients.notes: such as almond or soy milk

instructions: Cut the potato sticks into long, thin strips and soak them in cold water for at least 30 minutes to remove excess starch.
Drain the potato strips and pat them dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
In a shallow dish, mix together the cornstarch, Chinese five spice powder, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Pour the vegan batter mix into a separate shallow dish and stir in the vegan egg replacement and vegan milk until smooth.
Dip each potato strip into the batter mix, coating it evenly, then roll it in the cornstarch mixture to coat, tapping off excess.
Place the battered potato strips in a single layer on a plate or tray, leaving space between each fry.
Heat 1 cup of vegetable oil in an air fryer to 400°F (200°C) and cook the fries in batches until crispy and golden brown, about 10-12 minutes per batch.
Repeat with the remaining potato strips, adjusting cooking time as needed.
Serve the crispy battered fries hot, garnished with additional Chinese five spice powder if desired.
Garnish with fresh herbs, such as parsley or chives, for added color and freshness.
